name: Acme Construction V bayside school district

caseIntelligence.caseOverview: This matter involves a personal injury claim following a vehicle collision between Maria Gonzalez and Thomas Whitfield that occurred on November 3, 2023, at the intersection of SW 8th Street and Brickell Avenue, Miami, Florida. The demand letter drafted by Castillo & Associates, P.A., representing Gonzalez, accuses Whitfield of failing to yield at a red light, causing the accident, and demands a $250,000 settlement from State Farm Insurance Company. The letter outlines injuries, medical treatments, and damages incurred by Gonzalez. The demand letter stipulates a 30-day timeframe for settlement response before initiating legal proceedings.

unifiedTimeline.description: Vehicle collision between Maria Gonzalez and Thomas Whitfield
Hospitalization of Maria Gonzalez at Jackson Memorial Hospital for initial treatment
Discharge of Maria Gonzalez from Jackson Memorial Hospital
MRI confirmed a herniated disc at L4-L5 for Maria Gonzalez
Maria Gonzalez attended 24 physical therapy sessions
Completion of physical therapy sessions for Maria Gonzalez
Date of Demand Letter

caseIntelligence.caseOverview: This matter concerns the Bayshore Tower Condominium project in Miami, involving Meridian Construction Group, LLC and project design/consultant participants reflected in the RFI and change order records. The dispute centers on balcony waterproofing work that was installed in a manner that did not meet the manufacturer's requirements, including substrate curing prerequisites and detailing requirements, which led to removal and reinstallation work memorialized in Change Order No. 11. The documents indicate Meridian personnel, including Senior Superintendent Raymond Castillo, were aware of the membrane application problem in August 2022, with schedule pressure identified as a contributing factor and deficient non-conformance documentation admitted in deposition testimony. The current posture appears to be an active construction dispute or claim evaluation focused on responsibility for the noncompliant work, timing of knowledge, and resulting extra costs of $86,930.

unifiedTimeline.description: RFI No. 047 issued by Meridian regarding conflict between architectural balcony waterproofing detail and manufacturer requirements.
Architect Margaret Harlan responded to RFI No. 047, directing that the manufacturer's requirements govern, including minimum 28-day cure or 75% design strength before membrane application.
Concrete pour on floor 12 completed.
Scheduled start of waterproofing work on floors 11-14.

Daily superintendent log recorded that Coastal Waterproofing applied membrane on floors 12 and 13 before the concrete met the 28-day cure requirement or 75% design strength, and the project was approximately three weeks behind baseline schedule.
Concrete pour on floor 13 completed at 10:45 AM.

Raymond Castillo testified he was aware of the balcony slab waterproofing issue in August 2022, that schedule pressure contributed to early application, and that he did not prepare a non-conformance report or verify remediation.
Change Order No. 11 identifies the balcony waterproofing installation issue as having been identified by the superintendent on this date.

Change Order No. 11 issued for additional balcony waterproofing work requiring removal and reinstallation due to initial installation not meeting manufacturer requirements; amount $86,930 with no extension of substantial completion.

factConsistency.topic: Date waterproofing issue was first identified

documentA.statement: September 28, 2022: Installation issue identified by superintendent.

documentB.statement: Witness was aware of problems around August 2022 regarding membrane application before concrete cured.

factConsistency.explanation: These statements place the superintendent's awareness of the same waterproofing installation issue in materially different timeframes, which affects notice, responsibility, and characterization of when the problem was known.

factConsistency.topic: Issue identification date versus contemporaneous daily log

documentA.statement: September 28, 2022: Installation issue identified by superintendent.

documentB.statement: Coastal Waterproofing applied the membrane on floors 12 and 13 before the concrete cure time met the 28-day requirement or 75% design strength, potentially violating specifications per RFI No. 47.

factConsistency.explanation: The change order characterizes the issue as identified on September 28, while the daily log shows the same premature membrane application problem was observed and recorded on August 12.

documentA.statement: additional waterproofing work on balconies due to initial installation issues not meeting manufacturer requirements

documentB.statement: he did not agree with characterizations implying defective subcontractor installation

factConsistency.explanation: The change order frames the rework as resulting from noncompliant initial installation, while the superintendent disputed characterizations that the subcontractor's installation was defective.

caseIntelligence.caseOverview: The construction dispute involves the Palmetto Ridge Medical Office Building project, with Palmetto Ridge Development, LLC as the Owner and Keystone Building Group, Inc. as the Contractor. The nature of the dispute centers around delays attributed to late RFI responses, owner-directed design changes, and delayed owner-furnished equipment, resulting in Keystone claiming a 94-day contract extension and $1,847,000 in additional compensation. The Owner has denied the Claim based on timeliness and contractual terms. The dispute is currently in a contested phase with denial of the claim by the Owner.

unifiedTimeline.description: Contract executed by Owner and Contractor.
Notice to proceed issued, commencement of work.
Scheduled substantial completion date.
RFI-089 issued regarding structural conflict on Level 3.
Response to RFI-089 required by this date.
Architect's response to RFI-089 received.
RFI-089 closed.
Level 2 shell space conversion to imaging suite directive issued.
Revised elevator specifications issued impacting construction.
Relocation of primary electrical service entrance directive issued.
Project is 94 days behind schedule.
Notice of Claim letter dated.
Owner's Denial Letter issued.

documentA.statement: Keystone claims an extension of 94 days and additional compensation due to delays attributed to the owner and architect.

documentB.statement: The claim was submitted past the contractual 14-day notice period for events Keystone cited.

factConsistency.explanation: The Owner contends the Notice of Claim was not submitted within the required 14-day period, disputing its timeliness.

documentA.statement: Keystone claims an extension of 94 days and compensation of $1,847,000.00 due to delays.

documentB.statement: The owner disputes Keystone's delay claims and asserts errors in staff and process caused delays, not owner actions.

factConsistency.explanation: The Contractor claims delays caused by the Owner justify contract time extension and compensation, which the Owner disputes.
